reviewer: agent matrix pass (real headless inference + light/dark desktop+mobile screenshots read), revision 2

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/dfine-detection/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/dfine-detection/mobile.png

AGENT-VERIFIED real run (headless Chrome, WASM q8): clicked Download → auto-init → ready; Detect returned 271 candidate boxes above the 0.05 floor, 5 rows in the See-inside table, 3 count chips (cat/sofa/remote on sample-cats), labelled boxes drawn on the canvas at real pixel coords, latency 607 ms. Output shape = [{label, score, box:{xmin,ymin,xmax,ymax}}] as expected for object-detection; semantically correct for the sample.
rubric.notes: Confirmed real, semantically-correct D-FINE output on a real (headless) device — not manual-pending.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 5✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — declares-model-id=pass, declares-task=pass, declares-backend=pass, declares-quantisation=pass, inference-off-main-thread=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/dfine-detection/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/dfine-detection/mobile.png

rubric.notes: auto-checkable parts pass; manual-evidenced parts need an agent to Read the screenshot and confirm real output / controls / parity.

rubric.evidence: AGENT-VERIFIED: the visible boxes/labels come from the on-device pipeline run (count varied with the real image; latency 607 ms measured live; no hardcoded results anywhere in worker.js/dfine.js). Fresh-profile run showed the honest Download state before any output.
rubric.notes: No canned output — output is produced by the real transformers.js object-detection pipeline in the worker.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/dfine-detection/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/dfine-detection/mobile.png

AGENT-VERIFIED: read light+dark desktop (1280×800) and dark mobile (360×740, DPR3) screenshots — no horizontal overflow (measured 0px on mobile), no clipped controls, legible text and good WCAG-AA contrast in both themes. Ladder pages use flex with min-inline-size:0 and 44px select targets. Automated notes flag sub-24px targets = the range-slider thumb + informational count-chips, not primary actions.
rubric.notes: Usable + polished on both classes in screenshots; webcam/keyboard live-drive still relies on a real device (headless has no camera). Left at needs-review pending a full live control drive.

rubric.evidence: AGENT-VERIFIED: inference runs in a Web Worker (worker.js); the score slider filters a cached result (no re-run); the webcam loop schedules the next frame only after the last resolves and pauses on visibilitychange/pagehide (efficient-background-processing). Main-thread work (crop, draw) is bounded. No blocking loop observed; page stayed responsive during the 607 ms inference.
rubric.notes: INP kept low by off-thread inference + cached-slider filtering; no long main-thread task.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference runs off the main thread via a Web Worker (or MediaPipe delegate); control UI stays responsive.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid layout with min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; avoid fixed widths that overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run-it grid, ladder two-up columns, and inventory/crowd panels use flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0; canvases use inline-size:100% max-block-size. Measured 0px horizontal overflow at 360px.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive matrix desktop+mobile automated-clean; agent read screenshots; 0px overflow

guidanceConsulted.query: webcam getUserMedia inference loop without INP regression / pause when hidden
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Pause heavy background work (the webcam detect loop) when the tab/page is hidden.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the wild + main webcam loops stop on document.visibilitychange (hidden) and pagehide, and only schedule the next frame after the last detection resolves so work never piles up.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker-based loop + visibilitychange/pagehide handlers in index.html and wild/index.html

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible range slider and file dropzone, keyboard operable controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Labelled controls, keyboard-operable dropzone/samples, live status region.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — dropzone + sample thumbs are role=button with Enter/Space handlers, range sliders have <label>+<output>, loader status is role=status aria-live; conformance images-have-alt + buttons-have-names = pass.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance accessibility assertions pass; source review

openQuestions: Live webcam + keyboard focus order can't be exercised in headless (no camera) — confirm on a real device to flip support desktop/mobile from needs-review to ok.
Does the D-FINE vs RT-DETR race show the expected within-class latency (they share the decoder) across a range of real devices?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for dfine-detection: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm real output + tap targets + focus + dialogs, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence) and enrich this critique.

summary: Revision-2 agent matrix pass for dfine-detection (D-FINE SOTA real-time object detection, onnx-community/dfine_s_coco-ONNX, object-detection, WASM q8, ~10 MB). Verified REAL in-browser inference in headless Chrome: Download → auto-init → Detect returned 271 candidate boxes (5 See-inside rows, cat/sofa/remote chips) at 607 ms, console + network clean, 0px mobile overflow, light+dark legible. 14/23 conformance assertions auto-pass; the manual dimensions (io-shape, no-fake-output, performance) are now agent-confirmed from the real run. Support stays needs-review only because the live webcam loop + keyboard focus order need a real camera device to fully drive. Runnable-verified: transformers.js 3.7.5 registers DFineForObjectDetection + RTDetrImageProcessor, no version pin needed.
